title: Corporate Secretary Agreement
description: Outlines the duties, minutes preservation, and board support services provided by a corporate secretary.
whatsCovered: Scope of secretary duties, meeting minutes, and compliance
Corporate record-keeping and cap table tracking
whenToUse: Use this agreement when hiring or outsourcing your corporate secretary role to handle compliance filing, board minutes, and cap table audits.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

fields.label: Corporate Secretary Name / Agency
fields.placeholder: e.g. Apex Corporate Governance Services LLC

clauses.heading: CORPORATE SECRETARY AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Corporate Secretary Agreement. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Annual Retainer Fees
clauses.body: Secretary {secretary_name} shall manage corporate records and receive an annual service fee retainer of {secretary_retainer}.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Is the secretary a member of the Board?
faq.answer: The corporate secretary can be a board member, but is often an officer or third-party service provider who records minutes without voting.

faq.question: What records does the secretary maintain?
faq.answer: The corporate charter, bylaws, board meeting minutes, written consents, and shareholder registries.
